---
title: "AI / ML / MLOps Interview Handbook"
description: "A chronological, plain-English interview guide — from the math under machine learning to modern LLM agents — built as topic explainers plus simple question-and-answer drills."
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

A complete interview-prep handbook for **ML Engineer**, **MLOps Engineer**, and **AI Engineer** roles — rebuilt to be *understandable*, not just dense. It is ordered the way the field was actually invented: we **start with the mathematics** that everything rests on, walk forward through classical machine learning and deep learning, reach the **transformer** and the modern LLM, and end at today's frontier — **RAG, agents, and the loops** that wrap models into products. A final **practical-toolkit** set covers the libraries you get grilled on by name, closing with **deploying to the cloud** — AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and AI Studio.

Every topic follows the same shape: a short **plain-language explainer** (intuition first), then **simple question-and-answer drills** with simplified answers, and a diagram, formula, snippet of code, or small visual wherever it makes the idea click.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**How to use this:** each chapter is its own page (so it loads fast). Pick one below, read the explainers to build the model, then cover the answers and quiz yourself on the **Q:** lines. Every chapter page has *prev / next* links and a *jump-to-any-chapter* menu at the top.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 🗺️ Chapters — the chronological path {#roadmap}

**🧮 Mathematical Foundations** — *the bedrock every model stands on*

- [**01.** 🧮 Linear Algebra — the language of data](interview-guide/ch01.html)
- [**02.** 📉 Calculus & Optimization — how models learn](interview-guide/ch02.html)
- [**03.** 🎲 Probability & Statistics — reasoning under uncertainty](interview-guide/ch03.html)
- [**04.** 🔥 Information Theory & Loss Functions — measuring surprise and error](interview-guide/ch04.html)

**🧩 Classical Machine Learning** — *the algorithms that ran ML before deep learning*

- [**05.** 🧩 Core ML Concepts — the ground rules](interview-guide/ch05.html)
- [**06.** 📐 Classical Supervised Algorithms — the workhorses](interview-guide/ch06.html)
- [**07.** 🌲 Ensembles & Boosting — how to win on tabular data](interview-guide/ch07.html)
- [**08.** 🗺️ Unsupervised Learning & Dimensionality Reduction — structure without labels](interview-guide/ch08.html)
- [**09.** 🎯 Model Evaluation & Validation — knowing if it actually works](interview-guide/ch09.html)

**🧠 Deep Learning** — *neural networks, and how to actually train them*

- [**10.** 🧠 Neural Network Fundamentals — the building block](interview-guide/ch10.html)
- [**11.** ⚙️ Training Deep Networks — making deep nets actually train](interview-guide/ch11.html)
- [**12.** 🖼️ Convolutional Neural Networks — the vision branch](interview-guide/ch12.html)
- [**13.** 🔁 Sequence Models — RNNs, LSTMs and the bottleneck](interview-guide/ch13.html)

**⚡ The Transformer Era** — *embeddings, attention, and the architecture behind every LLM*

- [**14.** 🔤 Word Embeddings — giving words meaning as vectors](interview-guide/ch14.html)
- [**15.** ⚡ Attention & the Transformer — the architecture that changed everything](interview-guide/ch15.html)
- [**16.** 🧱 Tokenization, Pretraining & Model Families](interview-guide/ch16.html)
- [**17.** 📈 Modern LLMs & Scaling — bigger, and suddenly capable](interview-guide/ch17.html)

**💬 Using & Adapting LLMs** — *prompting, fine-tuning, RAG, and serving*

- [**18.** 💬 Prompting & In-Context Learning — programming models with words](interview-guide/ch18.html)
- [**19.** 🎚️ Fine-Tuning & Alignment — specializing and aligning models](interview-guide/ch19.html)
- [**20.** 📚 Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) — giving the model an open book](interview-guide/ch20.html)
- [**21.** 🚀 Inference, Decoding & Serving — running LLMs efficiently](interview-guide/ch21.html)

**🤖 The Agentic Frontier** — *agents, safety, and operating it all in production*

- [**22.** 🤖 Agents, Tools & Loops — the latest frontier](interview-guide/ch22.html)
- [**23.** 🛡️ Evaluation, Safety & Guardrails — making LLM systems trustworthy](interview-guide/ch23.html)
- [**24.** 🔧 MLOps & LLMOps — shipping and operating models in production](interview-guide/ch24.html)

**🛠️ The Practical Toolkit** — *the libraries and tools you will be asked about by name*

- [**25.** 🛠️ Practical Toolkit I — Modeling & Vision Libraries](interview-guide/ch25.html)
- [**26.** 🧰 Practical Toolkit II — LLM Frameworks, Orchestration & Vector Stores](interview-guide/ch26.html)
- [**27.** ⚙️ Practical Toolkit III — Serving, Apps & MLOps Tooling](interview-guide/ch27.html)

**☁️ Cloud AI Platforms** — *deploying foundation models on AWS, Google, and Azure*

- [**28.** ☁️ Cloud AI Platforms — deploying foundation models on the hyperscalers](interview-guide/ch28.html)
